text: +Easier to get up and get going in the morning. +Increased self esteem. +Increased confidence. +Some mood stabilization. +Significant decrease in skin-picking. +Increased overall energy. +Reduction in feelings of guilt and avoidance. +Enhanced social capacity: less inhibited, more likely to engage in conversation, more interested in others, less sensitive to surroundings, more open and increased assertiveness. +Night-time energy surge. +Reduction in taste sensitivity / food interest. +Late morning / early afternoon fatigue. +Occasional headaches. +Less organized thoughts / less aware of "details". +Hand tremors. +A.D.D. actually made WORSE. I am a recovering alcohol and drug user, and have A.D.D. and severe Depression, as well as possible undiagnosed Oppositional Defiant Disorder. I was taking Imipramine 100 mg. before bed and 20 mg. XR in the morning and 10 mg. in the afternoon before a hospitalization. I was switched to Prozac from the Imipramine, and given Trazadone to help sleep. I am also on an upward increase in dose of Strattera, because my A.D.D. has gotten very bad on the Prozac and off the Imipramine, but have a better mood and increased social ability.
